changes.value:
**Severity**: Medium (UI correctness)
**File**: `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` lines 1140-1152

The dispatch loop at line 1144 only runs `flush_and_emit_for_handle` when the command's `undoable` flag is true. The `app.undo` and `app.redo` commands are registered in the YAML command definitions. If their `undoable` field is `false` (or not set), the entity changes made by undo/redo will NOT trigger `entity-changed` / `entity-created` / `entity-removed` events.

The frontend UndoProvider refreshes undo state by listening to `entity-changed`, so if flush_and_emit is skipped, the UI will show stale entity data until the next unrelated event.

Verify: check the YAML command definition for `app.undo` and `app.redo` to confirm `undoable: true`. If not set, the fix is to add `undoable: true` to both command definitions.

Alternatively, the dispatch loop could special-case undo/redo to always flush, but that would be treating symptoms. #review-finding
Review finding: UndoCmd/RedoCmd flush_and_emit may not trigger for undo/redo results

--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1 +1 @@
-**Severity**: Medium (UI correctness)
**File**: `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` lines 1140-1152

The dispatch loop at line 1144 only runs `flush_and_emit_for_handle` when the command's `undoable` flag is true. The `app.undo` and `app.redo` commands are registered in the YAML command definitions. If their `undoable` field is `false` (or not set), the entity changes made by undo/redo will NOT trigger `entity-changed` / `entity-created` / `entity-removed` events.

The frontend UndoProvider refreshes undo state by listening to `entity-changed`, so if flush_and_emit is skipped, the UI will show stale entity data until the next unrelated event.

Verify: check the YAML command definition for `app.undo` and `app.redo` to confirm `undoable: true`. If not set, the fix is to add `undoable: true` to both command definitions.

Alternatively, the dispatch loop could special-case undo/redo to always flush, but that would be treating symptoms. #review-finding
\ No newline at end of file
+**Severity**: High (UI correctness -- confirmed bug)
**Files**: `swissarmyhammer-commands/builtin/commands/app.yaml` lines 7-19, `kanban-app/src/commands.rs` lines 1140-1152

CONFIRMED: Both `app.undo` and `app.redo` have `undoable: false` in `app.yaml`. The dispatch loop at line 1144 only runs `flush_and_emit_for_handle` when `undoable == true`.

This means after undo/redo:
1. Entity files ARE changed on disk (the Rust undo/redo logic works correctly)
2. But `entity-changed` / `entity-created` / `entity-removed` events are NOT emitted
3. The React UI shows stale data until the file watcher fires (race-dependent delay)

The `undoable: false` setting makes semantic sense (you don't want undo/redo operations to be undoable themselves -- that would create infinite recursion). But the dispatch layer conflates "this command should be tracked on the undo stack" with "this command mutates entity data and needs flush+emit".

Fix: The dispatch logic needs a separate flag or the flush gate needs to also trigger for `app.undo` / `app.redo` command IDs specifically. The cleanest fix is probably changing the flush gate to:
```rust
if undoable || cmd == "app.undo" || cmd == "app.redo" {
```

Or better: add a `mutates_entities: true` field to the command YAML that is independent of `undoable`. #review-finding
\ No newline at end of file
